mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.07% indicates deep accommodation regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals labor market strength and sustained consumer demand, driving positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations rise.
regime_hint: labor market expansion with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 150,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in real retail sales above the 3-month moving average indicates robust consumer spending and aggregate demand, which translates to positive corporate revenue growth and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: consumer-led expansion with rising discretionary spending
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when RSAFS exceeds its 3-month MA are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index below 105.0 signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side constraints, which compress profit margins and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances when INDPRO < 105.0, or Rank IC > -0.02 (insufficient negative correlation).

mechanism_narrative: A joint rise in unemployment rate above 6.0% combined with declining nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as recession risk increases.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 6.1%, current PAYEMS at 144,308
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with rising unemployment and job losses
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (UNRATE × PAYEMS decline) shows Rank IC < 0.03 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in housing starts above 1,700 thousand units combined with rising real retail sales signals broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as economic momentum accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1,739 thousand units, current RSAFS at 619,923 million
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumer spending
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when both conditions are met show Rank IC < 0.04, or SPX produces positive returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the Consumer Price Index above 0.5% combined with elevated federal funds rates above 2.0% signals monetary tightening pressure that raises real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.07%, well below threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressures
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows Rank IC > -0.03 (insufficient negative correlation) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly at levels above 2.5% annualized signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as profit expectations rise in expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 19,087.568 billion chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
